questions.question: A perfect copy of you appears — same memories, same habits, same loves. Is it you?

options.label: Yes — that continuity is all I am
No — a copy is a twin, not me
I genuinely can't tell

questions.question: If you could get away with anything — no one would ever know — would you still be good?

options.label: Yes — being just is worth it in itself
Honestly, no — we're good because we're watched
I'd like to say yes

questions.question: Could a machine ever really think — not simulate thinking, but think?

options.label: Yes — thinking is what it does, not what it's made of
No — there'd be nobody home
That's the wrong question

questions.question: Five people will die unless you sacrifice one. Is it ever right to do it?

options.label: Yes — five outweighs one
No — some things you may never do to a person
It depends on how, not how many

questions.question: Is beauty in the eye of the beholder?

options.label: Yes — there's no fact about what's beautiful
No — some things really are more beautiful
It's in the eye — but the eye can be trained

questions.question: This morning you chose what to wear. Could you actually have chosen otherwise?

options.label: Yes — the choice was genuinely open
No — it only felt open
No, but I was free in every way that matters

questions.question: The sun has risen every day of your life. Does that give you any reason to expect it tomorrow?

options.label: Of course — what else could?
Strictly? No — and that's unsettling
I've never once thought about it

questions.question: Is anything wrong everywhere — or is morality local custom, all the way down?

options.label: Some things are wrong everywhere
Custom, all the way down
I want the first, I suspect the second

questions.question: Do you owe the law obedience because you agreed to it — or because it can make you?

options.label: Because in some real sense I agreed
Because it can make me — that's all law is
I never agreed to anything

questions.question: Is your mind just your brain?

options.label: Yes — three pounds of matter, doing all of it
No — the mind is something else
It's the brain, but not JUST the brain

questions.question: Has anyone, anywhere, ever done a purely selfless thing?

options.label: Yes — real altruism exists
No — scratch it and you find self-interest
I keep going back and forth

questions.question: Can you be certain the world outside your head exists?

options.label: Yes — doubting it is a parlor game
No — and I can't prove otherwise
Certainty isn't the point

questions.question: Should the wisest rule — or should everyone get an equal say, wise or not?

options.label: The wisest — flying blind is not a virtue
Equal say — nobody's wisdom entitles them to rule me
Both answers frighten me

questions.question: Can a morally rotten artwork still be a great artwork?

options.label: Yes — greatness and goodness are different measures
No — the rot reaches the art
I love works I can't defend

questions.question: A tree falls with no one anywhere to hear it. Sound, or no sound?

options.label: Sound — the world doesn't need an audience
No sound — no hearer, no heard
Depends entirely what 'sound' means

questions.question: Is happiness the only thing good for its own sake?

options.label: Yes — everything else is good FOR something
No — a good life is more than a pleasant one
I can't name the alternative, but yes feels wrong

questions.question: Is knowledge just true belief you can back up — or can you be right for the wrong reasons?

options.label: Justified true belief — that's knowledge
You can be justified, true — and still just lucky
I need to see the trick first

questions.question: If a law is genuinely unjust, do you still owe it obedience?

options.label: Yes — order itself is worth something
No — an unjust law has no claim on me
Obey it while fighting to change it

questions.question: Your neurons are replaced one by one with chips doing exactly the same job. Are you still there at the end?

options.label: Yes — same functions, same me
No — somewhere in there I ended
I'd fade out and nobody would notice — including me

questions.question: Do you deserve blame for something you couldn't help doing?

options.label: No — blame needs a real alternative
Sometimes yes — it was still YOU doing it
No one deserves blame for anything, ever

questions.question: A machine offers you a perfect life you could never tell from real. Do you plug in?

options.label: Yes — how it feels is all that could matter
No — I want reality, not the feeling of it
How do I know I haven't already?
